name: Online physical activity did not change gut microbiome composition in healthy older adults
summary: A new healthy-older-adult RCT limits claims that short online-guided exercise reliably shifts gut microbiome composition in already fit older adults.
statement: In 92 healthy older adults randomized to an 8-week online-guided multicomponent physical activity intervention or active control, the intervention did not produce between-group differences in gut microbiome composition.
quantitative_note: The abstract reports no between-group differences in gut microbiome composition; exploratory cognitive and brain-connectivity signals were reported separately.
caveats: The authors note high baseline fitness may have limited observable change.
The intervention was short and online-guided, so it does not rule out other exercise doses, modalities, or lower-baseline-fitness populations.
The null result concerns microbiome composition and does not negate established non-microbiome exercise benefits.
